title: poa alpina subsp. subalpina
intro: Poa alpina subsp. subalpina, also known as the subalpine meadow grass, is a subspecies of the Poa alpina plant, which is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Poa alpina subsp. subalpina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, or its essential oil is extracted for use in aromatherapy and topical applications.
